'''The Dark Forces Saga''' was a series of six episodes of game scenarios released on the [[Wizards of the Coast]] web site. The episodes, written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Jason Fry]], were based on the characters in the popular [[Star Wars: Dark Forces|''Dark Forces'']] storyline and were intended to be played as scenarios of a tabletop role-playing game (RPG). The episodes provide story seeds, weapons stats, character stats and bios for the main characters, as well as background history for the planets of the Dark Forces story.

Peña and Fry later wrote a companion piece for ''[[Star Wars Miniatures]]'', ''[[Kyle Katarn's Tale]]''.

==Contents==
===''The Dark Forces Saga, Part 1''===
Published [[April 7]], [[2005]], provides a detailed biography of [[Kyle Katarn]], from his youth as a farmboy on [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]], to his disappearance during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], and everything in between. RPG stats for Kyle during the Yuuzhan Vong invasion are included, along with information about his trusty modified [[Bryar pistol/Legends|Bryar pistol]].

[[Rom Mohc]] is also covered, with background information and [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] RPG stats. His past during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], along with his fascination with [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], is revealed.

===''The Dark Forces Saga, Part 2: Unsung Heroes of the Light''===
Also published April 7, 2005, provides new and lengthy information about [[Jan Ors]] and [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]], as well as RPG stats for the two characters. Also given is an "adventure seed" based on Jan Ors and [[Mara Jade Skywalker]]'s post–''[[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|New Jedi Order]]'' search for Kyle. Kyle's ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'' is also given a backstory and statistics.

===''The Dark Forces Saga, Part 3: Pride of the Dark Side''===
Published [[May 12]], 2005, provides detailed information and statistics on [[Yun]] and [[Sariss]]. An "adventure seed" is provided, a scenario where Yun goes to [[Bunduki/Legends|Bunduki]] and encounters the [[Followers of Palawa]], and surprisingly, [[Arden Lyn]].

Notably, an old [[Decipher]] [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|SWCCG]] card entitled ''[[Prophet/Legends|Prophetess]]'' is [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] to be Sariss.

===''The Dark Forces Saga, Part 4: Of the Crudest Matter''===
Published [[May 26]], 2005, provides detailed backgrounds and RPG stats for [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]] and [[Boc Aseca]]. Two [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] combat styles, [[Trispzest]] and [[Jar'Kai/Legends|Jar'Kai]], are also described.

===''The Dark Forces Saga, Part 5: Two Peas in a Pod''===
Published [[June 9]], 2005, establish detailed backstories of [[Sithspawn/Legends|Sithspawn]] [[Gorc]] and [[Picaroon C. Boodle|Pic]], and their creation at the hands of Agent [[Blackhole]]. Detailed information and statistics regarding [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]] are also given.

Two adventure seeds are given. The first involves a mission where Kyle Katarn must retrieve [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda's]] lightsaber that was once in possession of Pic. The second, "Kyle Katarn, Meet Mara Jade," requires Kyle Katarn to rescue Mara Jade from Agent Blackhole.

===''The Dark Forces Saga, Part 6: Outcasts and Megalomaniacs Welcome''===
Published [[June 15]], 2005, focuses on the detailed background of [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]. It notably establishes that Jerec was once the Padawan of [[Jocasta Nu/Legends|Jocasta Nu]]. [[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast|''Jedi Outcast'']] antagonist [[Desann]] is also covered in great detail.

And finally, the [[Valley of the Jedi]] on [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] also gets its own treatment.

==Continuity==
Several retcons in ''The Dark Force Saga'' conflict with other [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] sources. Some examples are:

*'''The killing of Boc''': in ''The Dark Force Saga'' Kyle Katarn cuts Boc down with Yun's lightsaber, whereas in the ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' novella, Katarn kills Boc with a [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark-side]] technique called a [[Darkshear|spear of midnight black]] (which in turn contradicts the game, as that power isn't available to the player).
*'''Jerec as Miraluka''': ''The Dark Forces Saga'' specifies Jerec as a member of the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] species, while ''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' and ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' specified him as a [[Human/Legends|Human]].
*'''Sariss and Yun''': ''The Dark Forces Saga'' describes the relationship between Sariss and Yun such that she considers him a son, whereas in the ''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' novella, she does not have any motherly feelings for him, but rather considers him the closest thing to a friend she ever had.
*'''Gorc and Pic''': While they are visually the same species in both concept art and game, their new backstory states they are from different species, and with a mutation.
